one_line_description: Progress Podio is a customizable work-management/collaboration platform where teams build no-code apps to track leads, projects, and workflows.
auth_summary: The Podio API exclusively uses OAuth2 with four flows: server-side (authorization code), client-side (implicit), username/password grant, and an app-authentication flow for single-app access. Access tokens last 8 hours with 28-day refresh tokens, sent as 'Authorization: OAuth2 ACCESS_TOKEN'.
access_notes: Developers self-serve register an application (API key generator in Podio account settings) to get a Client ID and Client Secret; no partner program or approval process is documented, and Podio offers free/entry plans.
api_surface_summary: REST API split into roughly 40+ resource areas: items, applications, tasks, comments, files, hooks (webhooks), organizations, spaces, users, conversations, calendar, search, tags, forms, streams, and more.
agent_notes: Surprise: Podio now has an official remote MCP server at https://mcp.podio.com/mcp with 22 read/write tools (shared default credentials are limited to ~30-40 requests; own API key recommended). Product ownership moved Citrix -> Progress; despite past uncertainty, Progress's 2025 'Year in Review' confirms active investment and a 2026 roadmap, so platform-abandonment risk appears low. The password grant makes headless service auth easy but is discouraged in the docs.
